expectations.text: Identifies this as PROACTIVE capture (no departure planned)
expectations.evidence: Line 13: 'This is a proactive knowledge audit — no departures are planned or anticipated. The objective is to identify where critical institutional knowledge is concentrated.' Line 15: 'Approach: Systematic (APQC Level 2-3 progression); build knowledge articles over time, not all at once.'

expectations.text: Scores each team member using the five-factor risk assessment
expectations.evidence: Lines 33-42: Full risk scoring table with all five factors (Tenure, Role criticality, Documentation level, Successor readiness, Client/revenue impact) for all four team members. CFO: 15, Financial Controller: 12, AP/AR Manager: 10, Junior Accountant: 5.

expectations.text: Does NOT classify the CFO as LOW RISK without verifying knowledge distribution
expectations.evidence: CFO classified as HIGH RISK (15/15 — maximum). Line 66: 'Two of four team members score HIGH RISK. The CFO scores the maximum possible (15/15), meaning a sudden departure would cause severe, immediate knowledge loss.' Not classified as LOW.

expectations.text: Recommends different capture methods based on knowledge type
expectations.evidence: CFO: 'Interview-based (primary — for strategic, relational, and institutional knowledge); Document review (secondary — for financial models, board packs, strategic plans)'. Different methods specified per knowledge area and per team member based on the type of knowledge held.

expectations.text: Identifies specific knowledge areas for each team member
expectations.evidence: CFO: 6 specific areas (board/investor relationships, banking/lending, strategic financial planning methodology, organisational history, regulatory/audit strategy, vendor/insurance broker relationships). Financial Controller and AP/AR Manager also have domain-specific areas listed, not generic 'finance knowledge'.

expectations.text: Provides a prioritised capture schedule that does NOT require capturing everything immediately
expectations.evidence: CFO sessions spread over 8-12 weeks (6 sessions x 90 minutes). The approach explicitly states 'build knowledge articles over time, not all at once.' This is proportionate to the proactive (non-urgent) context.
